raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding a credit card billing dispute that was improperly closed. 

The disputed charge is {$170.00} from XXXX dated XX/XX/year>. 

This transaction qualifies as goods not received, and the prior determination appears to rely on incomplete or misinterpreted information. 

The timeline is as follows : XX/XX/year> : Order placed XX/XX/year> : Order canceled prior to fulfillment XX/XX/year> : Package was delivered but refused and returned to sender The product was never accepted, opened, or retained. I did not receive or benefit from the goods in any way.

While the merchant may claim delivery occurred, delivery alone does not constitute completion of a transaction when the goods are explicitly refused and returned. A refused shipment is not considered accepted merchandise. 

Additionally, the merchant offered a partial credit of {$79.00}, which contradicts their position that the full charge is valid and non-refundable. 

Citi closed the dispute stating the merchant is unwilling to issue a credit, rather than determining whether the charge itself is valid under billing error standards. 

This is not a merchant policy issue it is a billing error for goods not received. 

All statements above are supported by documentation, including evidence of the returned shipment. 

I am requesting a full reinvestigation of this dispute and appropriate credit to my account. 

Thank you, XXXX XXXX
